content: What exactly can be revived with Silver Core? Only Awakeners have Silver Core right? I only finished Chapter 7 and I been wondering since Chapter 1 with Aigis, who exactly can be revived
any silver core can theoretically be awakened if its gnostic index is above 21.
So it's something everyone has? Because at the end of Chapter 7, they mentioned they can't find William's Core so he can't be revived.
And I thought only Awakeners have that Core
since silver is a byproduct of dissolution (in theory), theres an chance that any living thing might form silver
theres a few cases where silver starts forming even while the person is alive
the thing with silver is that its rare, so more likely than not no silver is dropped upon death
if anything william just ended up with the most likely outcome, so he wasnt particularly unlucky
tldr; not everyone with silver is an awakener, but all awakeners have a silver core
Is the way Kathagu-ra treats the Keeper, despite seeing them in a similar light to her orphans, unique to them, or would she have been up to do anything with the orphans as well?

<@289320818901778434> Last time i did napkin math, i estimated that a Leonian Fang was worth about 1 shilling

So a florin could be the equivalent of a pound

assuming you're talking about her reproductive frenzy

Kath's relationship to her orphans is "youth gang leader." She is not an orphanage matron but a serial murderer and street vigilante.

Based on her side story, she and her gang all grew up together as teens, so I wouldn't be surprised if there was some fooling around going on

Kath's reproductive frenzy is a special case caused by the Holy River's influence though, I wouldn't assume that this is how she normally behaves
on the plus side, im sure hameln is incredibly dextrous with his fingers

> 茉夏，手游《雾境序列》中的五星斗士角色。隶属雾境调查团作战部先遣队。贝卢尔大学新生，因在雾灾中用钢管击杀古物展现特殊战力被招募，自称与调查团的相遇是“命运般的浪漫邂逅” [1] [4]。

Mouchette is a five-star fighter character in the mobile game "Mist Sequence". She belongs to the advance team of the combat department of the Mist Investigation Team. She is a freshman at Bellul University and was recruited because she used a steel pipe to kill an ancient artifact during the Mist Disaster. She claims that her encounter with the Investigation Team was a "romantic encounter of fate" [1] [4].

Question: I'm only at chapter four in the main story so it's very possible this is made clear later on or in one of the side stories, but when exactly does Doll begin to be influenced by a Mi-go? When I started the game I just assumed she was always a Mi-go just using a humanoid shell but there are lines where she seems to refer to that aspect as an 'Outer Will' influencing her. If so, did it just somehow hitch a ride during the Awakening process or what?

Doll was a normal human who was very smart and made a contingency plan for her own death to be resurrected as an awakener. She is not a mi-go (that's more like winkle)

All awakeners get their superpowers from eldritch entities which are vague and unspecified. When doll talks about an "outer will" she means the mysterious entity that gives her the powers of an awakener

we don't know much about how the outer wills correspond to awakeners other than they influence the form and power set of the awakener, and if you are especially close to one, you become a "Genesis" version which is more inhuman
You can sort of see this in ramona and her genesis version (ramona: timeworn)
We know specifically who ramona gets her powers from, it's tawil (or rather the eldritch entity using tawil as a proxy)
So was Doll before her Awakening different in personality then or did she just always have similar personality traits and tastes to the Mi-go?
from what her affinity stories say, her personality didnt change much. but after awakening, she could hear the voices of the mi-go
I guess that makes her akin to the 'special' humans the Mi-go picked out to bring back to Yuggoth via brain vat
because we can only observe the world from the inside, i.e, the perspective of the non-higher wills, we cant ascertain the nature of the higher wills upon Kadath either
what we do know is that at least in kadath, its considered dangerous to utter or reference their real names, so doll calls the mi-go alycymydes instead
